title: invoke.v1
description: The trigger that fires an agent run from a meeting. The transcript bridge subscribes the meetings transcript egress and, on a configured trigger, emits an Invocation that the agent-api turns into a runtime.v1 agent worker. This is the meetings → agent control-plane edge: a small, language-neutral envelope carrying WHY the run fires (the trigger) and WHICH meeting/workspace it acts on — never the transcript bytes themselves (those cross the meetings⊥agent seam as transcript.v1). No tenancy fields (deferred, ADR-0003).

Trigger.description: Why an agent run fires. `meeting.completed` = the session ended (post-meeting summary); `chat_invocation` = a user asked the agent something mid/after a meeting; `scheduled` = a runtime.v1 schedule fired.

MeetingRef.description: Which meeting this run is about — references the meetings domain by id, never by importing it.

Invocation.description: One agent-run trigger. Carries the trigger reason, the meeting it concerns, and the target user workspace the run should act on.

workspace_repo.description: the user workspace.v1 git repo URL the run commits to

workspace_ref.description: the branch the worker checks out

prompt.description: free-text ask — present for chat_invocation, null/absent for the others
